terms.definition: A partitioning clustering method that assigns each point to one of k clusters by minimizing within-cluster sum of squared Euclidean distances to centroids.
The standard k-means iteration: alternate assigning points to nearest centroids and updating centroids to cluster means until assignments converge.
The mean vector of all points assigned to a cluster; under squared Euclidean loss it is the optimal center for that fixed assignment.
Within-cluster sum of squares—the total squared Euclidean distance from each point to its assigned centroid; k-means minimizes this objective.
An initialization strategy that spreads initial centroids apart by sampling proportional to squared distance from existing centers, reducing poor local minima.
